mechanism: The olfactory systems of some flies help them find food due to their extreme sensitivity to the smell of rotting meat. Long-legged fly (Dolichopodidae) sitting on a leaf 1=Housefly sitting on a stretched electrical cable. Common house fly (''Musca domestica'') House flies mating (''Musca domestica'') Bildbeschreibung: Hausfliegenbein OLYMPUS DIGITAL CAMERA Larve einer Stubenfliege (Musca domestica (larva)) “The sense of smell is vital to most insects for finding food. Flies are particularly sensitive to the chemical odour given off by rotting meat…” (Foy and Oxford Scientific Films 1982:129)
function_addressed: sense chemicals (odor, taste, etc.) from the environment
